target.title: WhatsApp: send commentary live
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: WhatsApp suppressed assistant commentary-phase text entirely, so long-running agent tasks could look silent until the final answer.
- Why it matters: operators asked for opt-in live progress updates on WhatsApp without changing default delivery/privacy behavior.
- What changed: added opt-in `channels.whatsapp.commentaryDelivery = "live"` / account-level overrides, live text-only commentary delivery, final-reply dedupe for delivered commentary, abort/timeout bounds, docs, changelog, and Plugin SDK runtime documentation.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): commentary remains off by default, WhatsApp-only, text-only, and still suppressed for `message_tool_only` source-delivery routes.

AI-assisted: yes. I used Codex for implementation, local review, and validation, and addressed the local review findings before opening this PR.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #57484
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: current WhatsApp delivery treated assistant `commentary` phase as suppressed visible output with no opt-in channel delivery path.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no WhatsApp test covered live commentary extraction, text-only directive stripping, final-answer dedupe, silent-turn suppression, or source-delivery policy suppression.
- Contributing context (if known): closed PR #57484 had the original feature shape, but current `main` moved WhatsApp delivery ownership into `inbound-dispatch.ts` and changed runner fallback behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/pi-embedded-commentary.test.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.commentary.test.ts`, `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts`, `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/deliver-reply.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: live commentary is opt-in, text-only, silent-policy aware, source-delivery-policy aware, bounded by abort/timeouts, and not duplicated in the final reply.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: helper tests lock text extraction/dedupe; subscriber and WhatsApp dispatch tests lock the integration boundaries without requiring a live WhatsApp session.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing commentary suppression and block/final reply tests covered no-callback behavior but not live delivery.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Adds an opt-in WhatsApp config:

```json5
{
  channels: {
    whatsapp: {
      commentaryDelivery: "live",
    },
  },
}
```

The default remains `"off"`. Account-level `channels.whatsapp.accounts.<id>.commentaryDelivery` overrides the channel default.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
assistant commentary -> suppressed -> final reply only

After:
assistant commentary -> commentaryDelivery off -> suppressed
assistant commentary -> commentaryDelivery live -> text-only WhatsApp status message -> final reply dedupes delivered commentary
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: opt-in live commentary can send intermediate assistant status text over WhatsApp. It remains default-off, respects `silentExpected` and `message_tool_only`, strips/suppresses media/audio/reply directives, suppresses reasoning/tool/compaction payloads, and is bounded by abort/timeout handling.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local Node/pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: OpenAI Codex during live local testing
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp
- Relevant config (redacted): `channels.whatsapp.commentaryDelivery = "live"` during manual testing

### Steps

1. Enable WhatsApp `commentaryDelivery: "live"`.
2. Run a WhatsApp prompt that asks the agent to send commentary while doing work.
3. Observe commentary during the task, then final answer without duplicate commentary.

### Expected

- Live text-only commentary is delivered only when opt-in is enabled.
- Disabled/silent/message-tool-only routes do not deliver live commentary.
- Final replies do not duplicate already-delivered commentary.

### Actual

- Matches expected in local manual testing and targeted automated tests.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Automated validation:

- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-commentary.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.commentary.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/deliver-reply.test.ts`
- `pnpm -s tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm -s tsgo:core`
- `pnpm -s tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm build`
- `git diff --check`

Manual validation:

- Locally ran OpenClaw with the WhatsApp config and confirmed live commentary appeared during a task.
- Restored local config back to the globally installed OpenClaw version after testing.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: live WhatsApp commentary delivery, default-off behavior through tests, silent-turn suppression, source-delivery suppression, text-only directive stripping, final dedupe, abort/timeout bounded delivery paths.
- Edge cases checked: media/audio/reply directives in commentary, whitespace/silent commentary, account-level config override, malformed assistant content entries, partially delivered commentary suffixes, already-delivered commentary final suppression.
- What you did **not** verify: full `pnpm check` / `pnpm test` repository sweep; live WhatsApp testing after the final review-fix commit specifically.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

Local `ce-code-review` findings were addressed before opening this PR.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: no migration. Existing WhatsApp installs remain unchanged unless users add `commentaryDelivery: "live"`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: live commentary sends intermediate status text to WhatsApp.
  - Mitigation: default-off, text-only normalization, silent/source-delivery-policy gates, docs, and tests.
- Risk: commentary duplicates final answer.
  - Mitigation: delivered segment tracking plus final payload filtering/suffix preservation tests.
- Risk: commentary callback hangs a run.
  - Mitigation: abort signal propagation and bounded commentary delivery wait.
target.author: tristanmanchester
